text:
Market today: Geopolitical noise is the driver — Iran squeezing the Strait of Hormuz and Trump's tariff threats are pushing oil higher and adding macro uncertainty. That kind of risk-off backdrop hits growth/tech names hard, and you're margin-long in exactly those names.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 14.84%, your largest position, and on a TRIM signal. On margin, that hole gets deeper fast. PLTR is down 13.22% with a SELL signal — not a hold-through situation. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 11.61%; decay plus leverage plus margin is a dangerous combo, treat it as strictly tactical and size accordingly. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data — keep position small and watch it closely.

Watch: 1) Whether Iran-Hormuz escalation accelerates — sustained oil spikes tighten financial conditions and pressure tech multiples. 2) Trump's digital-services tariff threat — any broadening hits global tech sentiment directly.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers (AVGO, PLTR) are flashing actionable signals on margin — that's where your energy goes today.
